title: COM-Log v1.1
description: Machine-readable schema for the COM-Log output format. A COM-Log is the canonical structured output of a CAP analysis: a typed parsing of a life situation into domain, node, status, operator, and target.

domain.description: The primary domain of the situation.

node.description: The actual node (or nodes) hanging in this situation. Free-form but should reference typed node classes where possible.

current_status.description: Current status of the node. Multiple statuses can apply simultaneously (e.g. Open + Leaking).

outcome.description: Observed outcome that triggered the analysis. Used by Looking-Glass mode.

reverse_trace.description: Reverse-read reconstruction of the likely path leading to the current state. Looking-Glass output.

likely_split_point.description: Most likely critical transition where the route went costly or wrong. Diagnostic candidate, not certified history.

detected_operator.description: Operator the observer is currently applying (often the wrong one for this node).

missing_operator.description: Operator that should be applied but is not.

recommended_operator.description: The operator selection recommended by the system, with risk weight.

fallback_sequence.description: If RecommendedOperator is blocked by Telemetry, the lower-cost sequence to apply instead.

allowed_total_risk_percent.description: Computed AllowedTotalRisk = ObserverUsableBudget * RiskToleranceFactor. Bounds the sum of active operator risk weights.

budget_gate.description: Permission level for new operator chains.

target_status.description: Intended final status after the recommended operator is applied.

next_physical_step.description: Concrete physically, behaviorally, or procedurally executable step. Required. If a recommendation cannot be cashed out as a next physical step, it is not a CAP recommendation.

persistent_fault_candidate.description: True if this node has been Open or Leaking across 3+ consecutive audits and the same operator approach has not closed it.
